title: LATAM Quality Measurement & Reporting Gap
problem: Healthcare quality measurement in LATAM is immature. No standardized quality metrics. No public reporting of outcomes. Patients can't compare providers. No pay-for-performance systems. Quality improvement is ad-hoc. This limits value-based care adoption and allows poor-quality providers to persist.
solution: Healthcare quality measurement platform. Automated extraction of quality metrics from EMR. Risk-adjusted outcome reporting. Provider scorecards. Public transparency portal (like CMS Hospital Compare). Integration with payer value-based contracts. Quality improvement recommendations.
technology: EMR data extraction, NLP for unstructured data, risk adjustment algorithms, visualization dashboards, public portal
target_customer: Ministry of Health (public reporting), health insurers (VBC), hospital systems (quality improvement), accreditation bodies
ai_type: NLP for clinical data extraction, quality prediction
traction_signals: Value-based care emerging in private insurance
regulatory_notes: Quality reporting regulations, data standardization requirements
varden_fit: HIGH FIT - Quality measurement is infrastructure for value-based care. B2B/B2G software. Long-term contracts with government and insurers. Defensible through data assets.
notes: CONTRARIAN + BOREALING: Quality reporting is invisible infrastructure. But it's essential for value-based care. Government contracts are large and stable. Very boring but very valuable.
